title: Lesson 97: (Grammar) - be; is; did; (have) done; please; do

intro_cards.title: Plain Copula: だ
intro_cards.body:
だ is the plain/casual form of です — used in informal speech and as base for many grammar patterns.

• これは本だ。(This is a book.)
• 彼は先生だ。(He is a teacher.)

Plain form chart for nouns/な-adj:
• Present: だ / Negative: じゃない
• Past: だった / Past neg: じゃなかった

Note: だ sounds direct/blunt. Use です for polite speech.
だ is required before certain particles:
• 静かだから (because it's quiet)
• 元気なの？(Are you well? — note: な before の/か)
